howItWorks.intro: This tie-dye calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Fabric = Number of Items × Average Weight
Dye per Color = (Total Lbs × Dye Rate) ÷ Number of Colors
Soda Ash = ~1 cup per gallon of soak water
Dye rates: Light 0.5 tsp/lb, Medium 1.5, Dark 3, Black 6 tsp/lb
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much dye do I need per shirt?
faq.answer: A medium-weight t-shirt (6 oz) needs about 0.5-1.5 teaspoons of fiber reactive dye total across all colors for medium saturation. Use more for dark colors, less for pastels.

faq.question: What is soda ash and why do I need it?
faq.answer: Soda ash (sodium carbonate) raises pH to activate fiber reactive dyes, creating a permanent chemical bond with cotton fibers. Without it, colors wash out. Soak items for 15-20 minutes before dyeing.

faq.question: How long should I let tie-dye sit?
faq.answer: Let wrapped items cure for 12-24 hours at room temperature (minimum 70°F). Longer curing = more vibrant colors. Keep items damp during curing by sealing in plastic bags or wrap.

faq.question: Is the Tie-Dye Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard crafting and DIY formulas trusted by crafters and makers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Tie-Dye Calculator?
sections.body: The Tie-Dye Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your number of items, avg item weight, number of colors, you get instant results including total fabric weight, dye powder per color, soda ash needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total fabric weight right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your number of items and need to find the right total fabric weight. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
